results.title: Addressing Cost Increases Resulting from New Lift and Escalator Licensing Regime for Lift and Escalator Certification Firms
results.summary: From 2027, the Building and Construction Authority (BCA) will enforce a new licensing regime requiring developers and building owners to engage licensed firms for lift and escalator certification to enhance safety and align with international practices. The Government provides substantial funding to Town Councils to offset estate maintenance costs, while advising them to prudently factor expected cost increases from Fixed Installation plan submissions, certifications, and annual inspections into their budgeting. A competitive pool of licensed firms is being cultivated to help maintain cost competitiveness for lift owners.

results.title: ActiveSG Credits for Facility Bookings under Dual-Use Scheme
results.summary: The Government confirmed that SportSG is working on enabling ActiveSG credits for school facility bookings under the Dual-Use Scheme and will provide an update when ready. Regarding payment inclusivity, MyActiveSG+ launched last year to allow PayNow and debit card usage for bookings, assisting families without credit cards; those unable to pay may use remaining credits or seek assistance from SportSG Champions at Sports Centres. For lower-income households, SportCares provides free programmes like Fun Starters and Saturday Night Lights, alongside sport bursaries for ActiveSG Academies & Clubs. ActiveSG credit top-ups include $100 for new members since 2014, an additional $100 for children aged 4-12 in 2023, and $100 SG60 credits for all Singaporeans in 2025.

results.title: Addressing Business Impact of Jetstar Asia's Relocation from Changi Airport and Better Connectivity between Terminal 4 and Other Terminals
results.summary: Jetstar Asia ceased operations on 31 July 2025, accounting for approximately 3% of Changi's passenger traffic prior to closure. The Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore and Changi Airport Group are enhancing capacity on affected routes, with three of the four former Jetstar routes now or soon to be served by other carriers. To improve Terminal 4 connectivity, Changi Airport Group will increase shuttle bus frequency between Terminal 4 and other terminals starting November 2025 as part of ongoing passenger experience enhancements.

results.title: Assessing ASEAN's Capacity to Resolve Conflicts without External Intervention
results.summary: The Minister stated that ASEAN has successfully shifted regional relationships from conflictual to cooperative since 1967 by fostering dialogue and anchoring external partners. In the Cambodia-Thailand case, Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim, as ASEAN Chair, brokered a ceasefire in July 2025 with support from the United States and China, followed by a General Border Committee meeting on 10 September 2025. Existing dispute settlement mechanisms under the ASEAN Charter and the Treaty of Amity and Cooperation allow for conciliation or mediation through the Chair or Secretary-General, operating on a consensus principle. Singapore does not plan to propose establishing a new permanent ASEAN-led body for mediation, preferring to encourage direct engagement between conflicting parties.

results.title: Assessing Relevance of 2,000 kg Weight Limit for Season Parking of Commercial Vehicles
results.summary: The Ministry maintains that commercial vehicles with an unladen weight exceeding 2,000 kilograms are prohibited from parking in HDB residential car parks due to safety concerns regarding structural capacity, vehicle size, and obstruction risks. While heavier electric commercial vehicles are increasingly prevalent, the assessment of the weight limit remains unchanged. Commercial vehicles exceeding this limit may apply to park at HDB surface residential car parks on a case-by-case basis or utilize commercial, industrial, and purpose-built heavy vehicle parks. Agencies are currently reviewing structural enhancements to accommodate heavier vehicles in the future.

results.title: Assessment on Leasing of Fast-food Business at Yio Chu Kang Stadium to Promote Healthy Living
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed that all food and beverage tenants at ActiveSG Sport Centres, including fast-food businesses at Yio Chu Kang Stadium, must comply with the Whole-of-Government healthier F&B policy. Operators are required to provide Nutri-Grade A or B beverages, use healthier oils and lower-sodium ingredients, or obtain Health Promotion Board certification for dishes lower in calories, sodium, and higher in wholegrains. These requirements must be met before SportSG leases the spaces, ensuring convenient dining options support Singaporeans in adopting healthy and active lifestyles.

results.title: Average Carbon Tax Rates and Impact of Allowances on Reducing Carbon Emissions
results.summary: The average carbon tax rate for taxable facilities from 2019 to 2023 was $5 per metric tonne of carbon dioxide, with 2024 collections still being finalised. Allowances to defray this tax were introduced in 2024, requiring recipient companies to submit credible net-zero decarbonisation plans that the Government reviews and holds them accountable to. The Minister did not provide specific data on how these allowances supported emission reductions in previous years, as the allowance scheme is a new measure.

results.title: Average Waiting Time for Late-stage Cancer Patients to Commence Chemotherapy in Public Hospitals
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track average waiting times for chemotherapy in public hospitals, stating that treatment timing depends on clinical considerations rather than a fixed metric. The Minister confirmed there are no recorded instances where late-stage cancer patients faced denial or significant delays specifically due to resource constraints. Healthcare providers continue to collaborate with patients to optimise treatment plans while ensuring appropriate and timely care within the existing public healthcare system.

results.title: Braking Procedures for Bus Captains to Ensure Safety of Elderly and Pregnant Commuters
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that bus captains are required to wait for vulnerable passengers, including the elderly and pregnant, to be seated or holding a handrail before driving off. This safety protocol applies alongside existing training to avoid harsh braking and the use of telematics systems to monitor driving behaviour. The Minister noted that captains must also respond to road conditions and other drivers, meaning they cannot always spot passengers moving between stops. Consequently, passengers are urged to remain seated and refrain from moving about the bus until it comes to a complete stop to ensure safety.

results.title: Breakdown of GRTP Beneficiaries by Educational Qualifications over Past Five Years
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, the Global Ready Talent Programme (GRTP), administered by EnterpriseSG, supported over 29,000 students through local and overseas internships. The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry provided a breakdown of beneficiaries by educational qualification in Table 1, which lists the number of degree, diploma, and NITEC or Higher NITEC students rounded to the nearest ten. The specific figures for each educational profile are detailed in the referenced table.

results.title: Building Covered Linkways to Connect HDB Blocks, Amenities and MRT Stations Beyond 400-metre Radius
results.summary: In 2024, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) announced plans to construct covered linkways connecting MRT stations to designated Friendly Streets and key amenities within an 800-metre radius. While the Ministry of Transport (MOT) prioritizes walkways linking commuters to major transport nodes and amenities based on usage patterns, it will also evaluate proposals for connections to Housing and Development Board blocks. However, LTA will consider site constraints and safety requirements alongside human traffic flow when assessing such requests. The agency commits to prioritizing routes serving large numbers of commuters and will incorporate community feedback, with special attention to the needs of vulnerable commuters.

results.title: Building In-house Dialysis Facilities in More Public Nursing Homes
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) does not mandate in-house dialysis facilities in public nursing homes, noting that only a small proportion of residents currently require such treatment. Consequently, MOH considers it more cost- and manpower-efficient for these clients to receive care at community dialysis centres. However, some public nursing homes are co-located with community dialysis centres to meet sufficient local demand from nursing home residents and nearby patients. The Ministry will not incorporate in-house dialysis facilities into more public nursing homes as a standard measure.

results.title: Causes of Bukit Panjang LRT Disruptions in July 2025
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the causes of the two Bukit Panjang LRT disruptions in July 2025 were addressed in Oral Parliamentary Questions Nos 19 to 24 during the 22 September 2025 Parliament Sitting. No new learning points, design flaw assessments, or engineering expertise shortages were provided in this response. The specific details regarding the disruptions remain available in the referenced parliamentary record.

results.title: Clarification by Senior Minister of State for Manpower
results.summary: Senior Minister of State for Manpower corrected a previous statement regarding complaints about illegal platform workers received by the Ministry of Manpower (MOM). The accurate figure is approximately 50 complaints per year since January 2023, rather than the initially stated 70. This clarification addresses the volume of complaints prior to recent enforcement actions. No further details on enforcement outcomes or specific programme impacts were provided in this exchange.

results.title: Committee of Selection
results.summary: The appointment of the Committee of Selection enables it to nominate Members for six other Standing Select Committees and the Special Select Committee on Nominations for Appointment as Nominated Members of Parliament. This procedural step initiates the selection process for these specific parliamentary bodies. No further details regarding timelines, eligibility criteria, or specific nomination counts were provided in the transcript. The focus remains strictly on the activation of the Committee's nominating function for the listed select committees.

results.title: Complaints Resolution and Regulatory Actions against Debt Collection Agencies
results.summary: Since the Debt Collection Act commenced on 1 December 2023, eight cases of unlicensed collection by agencies or individuals have been identified. Police issued stern warnings to three companies involved in these activities while investigations continue for the remaining cases. None of the eight cases involved debts owed to public hospitals, and the Police do not track customers of debt collection companies. No specific disciplinary actions beyond warnings were detailed in the response.

results.title: Considerations for Fish Farms Production Quotas
results.summary: The Singapore Food Agency (SFA) establishes fish production quotas to ensure farms optimise food production sustainably within environmental limits. Kelongs, defined as small-scale coastal platforms capturing wild fish, have not received new licences since 1965 due to declining catches. SFA will continue partnering with farmers to increase productivity through the Agri-Food Cluster Transformation Fund for technology adoption and Aquatic Animal Health Services for biosecurity. Account managers regularly engage farms to understand needs and provide appropriate support. The Minister did not specify new measures for struggling kelongs beyond existing support frameworks.

results.title: Criteria for "Heartland Merchants" to be Eligible for CDC Vouchers Scheme
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth clarified that the CDC Voucher Scheme targets hawkers and merchants in HDB heartlands, including National Environment Agency-managed centres, to support daily goods and services. While strict criteria exist, small businesses in areas with few or no local merchants are onboarded on a case-by-case basis since October 2021. To date, over 23,000 hawkers and heartland merchants have been onboarded, including those who successfully appealed. The Ministry continues collaborating with partners like the Federation of Merchants' Association to identify eligible merchants, though specific current eligibility criteria and application statistics for 2024 to 2025 were not disclosed in the response.

results.title: Criteria in Appeals For Waiver of Minimum Occupation Period for HDB Flat Sales
results.summary: HDB assesses appeals for Minimum Occupation Period (MOP) waivers on a case-by-case basis, considering specific circumstances such as financial hardship or the demise of the owner. The Minister confirmed that the remaining MOP duration is not a stated criterion for these exceptions. All appeals are evaluated individually to determine eligibility for selling flats on the open market before the standard MOP expires.

results.title: Data On Foreigners Working Without Valid Employment Passes
results.summary: From 2020 to 2024, an average of 450 foreigners were found working illegally annually, with 13 prosecuted, four fined, and the remainder warned or repatriated. The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) collaborates with agencies like Vicpa to address hotspots in services and construction sectors, where errant employers face fines up to $30,000 or 12 months' imprisonment under the Employment of Foreign Manpower Act. Enforcement prioritizes warnings for misled workers after investigations, while proactive and reactive inspections target high-risk areas based on whistleblower tips or complaints. Specific sectors like wedding shoots see roughly two cases yearly, prompting joint investigations with industry associations to deter illegal employment without penalizing vulnerable workers.

results.title: Data on Reported Abuses of Training Employment Pass Scheme
results.summary: In 2023 and 2024, 230 employers were investigated for abusing the Training Employment Pass (TEP) scheme, involving 520 TEP holders. The Minister for Manpower confirmed these figures in response to a query regarding reported abuses since the beginning of 2023. The response provided specific counts for investigations and affected trainees but did not detail the specific nature of the abuses or any subsequent penalties imposed on the employers.

results.title: Data on School Bus Fares for Primary School Children and Ensuring Affordability of Fares
results.summary: School bus fares for primary students are determined via competitive bidding with contracts lasting two years or more. For 2025 contracts, average fare increases were 7% for trips up to 2km and 1% for longer trips. Low-income families receive subsidies under the MOE Financial Assistance Scheme, which rose from 60% to 65% of monthly fares in January 2023, with schools providing further support as needed. To maintain affordability, the Government exempts school buses from Certificate of Entitlement requirements, offers a 50% road tax rebate to eligible operators via the Land Transport Authority, and provides additional foreign worker quotas for driver recruitment. MOE will continue monitoring fares and step up financial support if necessary.

results.title: Data on Vehicle Fire Incidents to Date and Analysis of Causes
results.summary: In the first eight months of 2025, Singapore recorded 152 vehicle fires. The Singapore Civil Defence Force does not track whether vehicles were stationary or in operation at the time of incidents. Primary causes identified include engine overheating, electrical faults, and ignition sparks resulting from collision impacts. Consequently, specific data on stationary versus operational vehicle fires is unavailable.

results.title: Deploying Improved Weather Forecasting Developed by Climate and Weather Research Alliance in Military, Aviation and Maritime Sectors
results.summary: The Climate and Weather Research Alliance Singapore (CAWRAS), launched in September, will deploy improved weather forecasting using high-resolution modelling and artificial intelligence to address tropical weather complexities. The first projects are scheduled for completion by 2029, with progressive improvements to be integrated into Meteorological Service Singapore's services. These enhancements will specifically benefit the military, aviation, and maritime sectors by improving operational safety for air traffic and vessel management, optimising cargo handling, and mitigating risks to servicemen from adverse conditions like heavy rainfall and heat stress. The initiative aims to provide timely data to empower stakeholders in proactively managing weather-related risks across these critical sectors.

results.title: Difference between Functions of ComLink Family Coaches and Family Service Centre Social Workers
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Family Service Centre (FSC) case workers support vulnerable families with higher needs, such as conflict or emotional issues, while Social Service Office (SSO) family coaches assist those with lower needs. For families eligible for ComLink+, FSC workers act as coaches but prioritize addressing higher risks first before providing other ComLink+ support. Conversely, SSO-coached families developing higher needs are referred to FSCs, and those no longer requiring FSC support are transferred back to SSOs to continue goal-focused coaching. This referral system prevents duplication and ensures coordinated case management across both agencies.

results.title: Effectiveness and Adequacy of School Counsellors and Factors Determining Expansion
results.summary: The Minister for Education directed members to a written reply dated 23 September 2025 regarding the review timeline, expansion indicators, and equity measures for school counsellors. The response also addressed the evaluation of the current mental health ecosystem, which includes trained counsellors, flexi-adjunct counsellors, teacher-counsellors, and peer support networks. Specific details on the review process, expansion triggers, and barriers to help-seeking were not provided in this oral session but are available in the referenced written reply. Members are advised to consult Question No 70 in the 23 September 2025 Order Paper for the full policy data.

results.title: Effectiveness of New Towns' Public Spaces in Promoting Outdoor Activity and Family Bonding in Line with Grow Well SG
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) designs new town public spaces, such as Tengah's thematic playgrounds and green connectors, to promote outdoor activity and family bonding under Grow Well SG. These facilities integrate 3-Generation concepts by co-locating childcare, senior care, and fitness stations to foster inter-generational interaction. Effectiveness is evaluated through Sample Household Surveys conducted every five years, Post-Occupancy Surveys for new Build-To-Order developments, and observational studies. Insights from these assessments, alongside research collaborations, inform continual improvements to ensure spaces evolve with changing family needs and support healthy lifestyle habits.

results.title: Effectiveness of Single Room Shared Facilities Scheme in Supporting Homeless Individuals
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) states that both the Single Room Shared Facilities (SRSF) and Joint Singles Scheme Operator-Run (JSS-OR) pilots effectively assist rough-sleeping singles by providing public rental options managed by social service agencies. While SRSF offers a private bedroom with shared facilities and JSS-OR involves sharing a 1- or 2-room flat, both models are well-subscribed with generally positive feedback. HDB is currently reviewing these public rental typologies and will share further details, including any expansion plans for SRSF, when ready. The Minister did not provide a specific assessment comparing the two schemes' effectiveness or a confirmed timeline for SRSF expansion.

results.title: Enforcement of Traditional Election Advertising Rule During 2025 General Election
results.summary: During the 2025 General Election, the Elections Department received 2,561 reports on potential contraventions of traditional election advertising (TEA) regulations, with 1,840 requiring further investigation. For 1,595 reports lodged between Writ Day and Cooling-off Day, candidates were notified to rectify breaches within three hours; however, 100 unrectified cases resulted in enforcement action. On Polling Day, due to inclement weather, no rectification notifications were issued. Instead, ELD conducted on-site verification and directly removed 88 TEA found in breach, bringing the total removed from Writ Day to Polling Day to 227. A breakdown of these removals by political party is provided in the accompanying table.

results.title: Enhancement of Annual Disbursement of LifeSG Credits under Large Families Scheme to Eligible Children
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the Large Families Scheme (LFS) currently provides $1,000 annual LifeSG credits per third and subsequent child from age one to six, based on research indicating higher needs for infants and toddlers. While significant support exists for children beyond six years through Child Development Account co-matching, education subsidies, and tax benefits, the specific proposal to enhance annual disbursements for children aged seven to 12 was not addressed. Instead, Budget 2025 introduced a separate measure granting all Singapore Citizen children aged zero to 12 a flat $500 in Child LifeSG Credits. The Government stated it will continue monitoring the LFS and consider adjustments where necessary.

results.title: Ensuring Alignment of Training Initiatives with Emerging Skill Needs in Fourth Industrial Revolution
results.summary: The Minister stated that the Ministry aligns training initiatives with Industry 4.0 needs through the Advanced Manufacturing Training Academy (AMTA), which scans emerging developments and coordinates with training providers to refresh curricula. Institutes of Higher Learning offer refreshed programmes covering Industry 4.0 technologies at Nitec, diploma, undergraduate, post-diploma, and Masters levels, while SkillsFuture supports existing professionals with approximately 350 shorter upskilling programmes attended by 11,300 individuals last year. Additionally, about 330 manufacturing companies have benefited from SkillsFuture Queen Bee networks, where leading firms like Siemens mentor local organisations to adopt new technologies through training and co-developed proof-of-concept projects. Companies are urged to utilise these channels, including direct engagement with educational institutions or AMTA, to develop provisions that prepare the workforce for emerging skill needs.

results.title: Ensuring Bus Stop Shelters Provide Adequate Roof Coverage during Heavy Rain
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority regularly reviews bus stop designs to improve commuter protection, incorporating back panels that function as rain screens in new constructions. When older stops are upgraded, these enhanced features are adopted, and higher-traffic locations, such as those connecting to MRT stations, may receive wider roofs if infrastructure permits. The Authority assesses existing shelters to ensure adequate coverage during heavy rain, prioritizing retrofits in busy areas where feasible.

results.title: Ensuring Commercial Rent Affordability for SME Tenants at JTC and HDBs
results.summary: JTC and HDB maintain affordable commercial rents for SMEs through frameworks ensuring rates are competitive, with JTC industrial rents averaging 10% below market and 90% of HDB commercial properties seeing no rent increases in five years. Both agencies offer staggered rent adjustments for eligible lease renewals, while HDB provides specific rental discounts for startups and social enterprises. Although JTC and HDB do not impose controls on tenant mix by business size or ownership, the majority of their tenants are currently SMEs. The Government reconciles SME support with commercial operations by continuing holistic assistance via the Enterprise Financing Scheme, Enterprise Development Grant, and Productivity Solutions Grant to share loan risks and upgrade productivity.

results.title: Entry Restrictions and Enforcement Actions for Foreign-registered Vehicles with Outstanding Offences over Past Three Years
results.summary: Foreign-registered vehicles exceeding a specific threshold of outstanding fines face entry denial into Singapore, with fines required to be settled before re-entry is permitted. Entry denials totaled 149 in 2022, 174 in 2023, and 1,018 in 2024. As of August 2025, approximately 58% of fines issued during this three-year period remain unpaid. The Minister confirmed these enforcement actions apply to traffic, parking, and emission-related offences, ensuring prompt settlement of penalties incurred while driving on Singapore roads.

results.title: Establishing Communication Standards for Public Announcement Timelines of Train Disruptions
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the question regarding standards for maximum announcement timelines for train disruptions has already been addressed in Oral PQs 19 to 24 during the 22 September 2025 Parliament sitting. No new measures, timelines, or operational constraints were introduced in this session. The specific details of the previous responses remain the authoritative source for this inquiry. Further clarification on the established standards was not provided in this transcript.

results.title: Evaluating Effectiveness of Enforcement on Pedestrian-only Paths and Cycling Lanes
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) evaluates enforcement effectiveness through active patrols by officers who issue warnings or fines for intentional rule violations, particularly on pedestrian-only paths (POPs) effective from 1 July 2025. Since that date, approximately five fines have been issued for errant riders who blatantly disregard path segregation without safe regard for others. Safety enhancements include installing convex mirrors, improving lighting, and trimming hedges, alongside regular education campaigns targeting schools, community groups, and delivery platforms. While bicycle registration was discontinued due to administrative burdens, the Ministry maintains that third-party liability insurance covers work-related rides and that educational measures remain the sustainable approach for promoting safe shared path usage.

results.title: Evaluating Success of Pulau Tekong Polder Project and Assessment of Long-term Viability
results.summary: The Pulau Tekong polder project generated approximately 800 hectares of new land, achieving nearly 50% savings in sand usage compared to traditional reclamation. The coastal dike, drainage network, and stormwater systems are designed to support climate resilience against rising sea levels and intense rainfall, with the dike height adjustable for future increases. While currently allocated for Ministry of Defence training, the land could potentially accommodate residential, commercial, or industrial uses on the mainland. Maintenance costs are being monitored until the project reaches a steady state. The Minister did not specify a formal policy framework for evaluating success or a definitive timeline for alternative land uses.

results.title: Expanding Legal Aid Schemes for Financially Constrained Persons Beyond Current Pro Bono Eligibility
results.summary: The Minister for Law stated that the Legal Aid Bureau (LAB) provides civil and family legal aid to citizens and permanent residents meeting 2024 means test criteria of $1,050 per capita household income and $21,000 annual residence value. LAB exercises flexibility for deserving cases via a Minister-appointed panel to grant aid to those facing extenuating circumstances despite marginal non-compliance. Individuals ineligible for government schemes may access advice at Community Law Centres through Pro Bono SG (PBSG) or seek representation under specific schemes like the Family Justice Support Scheme and Ad Hoc Pro Bono Scheme for exceptional cases. The Government maintains a partnership with the legal fraternity and civil society to enhance access to justice for vulnerable Singaporeans.

results.title: Extending Global Talent Ready Programme to MNCs without 30% Local Shareholding Requirement
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister clarified that the Global Talent Ready Programme maintains a 30% local shareholding requirement to support Singapore enterprises, aligning with other business grant schemes. While this criterion excludes foreign multinational corporations without local ownership, alternative internship pathways exist through the Infocomm Media Development Authority's co-funded initiatives with international technology firms and Institutes of Higher Learning. These alternative arrangements enable students to gain global exposure and industry-relevant skills without requiring the specific programme extension requested. The Minister noted that Institutes of Higher Learning also independently secure sufficient internships with both local and foreign firms to meet course requirements.

results.title: Ensuring Delivery Platform Work is Performed by Singaporeans and Permanent Residents Only
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) and National Environment Agency (NEA) will ensure delivery platform work is performed exclusively by Singaporeans and Permanent Residents (PRs) through strict enforcement rather than increasing administrative burdens on companies. While platforms must track subcontractors and foreign work pass holders, reporting requirements for outsourced workers will be annual rather than monthly or quarterly to balance compliance with operational dynamics. Enforcement will prioritize random, frequent facial verification checks and impose stiff penalties for violations, with MOM and NEA collaborating to identify hotspots and conduct ground-level actions. The government aims to deter illegal foreign employment by imposing onerous demands without compromising stakeholder capacity, ensuring local workers' income opportunities remain uncompromised by outsourcing.

results.title: Extending Operating Hours of Johor-Singapore Rapid Transit System beyond 6.00 am to 12.00 am
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that RTS Link operating hours are mutually agreed upon between Singapore and Malaysia, the operator (RTS Operations Pte Ltd), and the operator. Extensions beyond 6.00 am to 12.00 am depend on ridership demand and operational constraints. No specific timeline or commitment for the proposed extension was provided. The Ministry will consider requests based on these factors rather than unilaterally setting new hours.

results.title: Extending Use of Pro-family Leave to Provide Caregiving to Spouses
results.summary: The Ministry for Education confirmed that teachers are already entitled to pro-family leave to care for spouses, aligning with updated Civil Service provisions effective earlier this year. This entitlement is not a new measure but an existing provision previously available for parents and children. The Minister clarified that leave for spouse caregiving is included within the standard leave framework rather than being classified as no-pay leave. Consequently, the specific request to extend this category is unnecessary as the policy already permits it.

results.title: Further Measures in Managing Rising Crow Populations in Housing Estates
results.summary: The Minister for National Development directed that the question regarding further measures to manage rising crow populations in housing estates, including the efficacy of scaling up effigy trials, be addressed via a written reply. This response was issued as Question No. 44 on the Order Paper dated 23 September 2025. No specific operational measures, timelines, or statistical outcomes were stated in the parliamentary record. The substantive details of the NParks' considerations and trial results remain contained within the referenced written answer rather than the oral transcript.

results.title: Guidelines for Academic Misconduct and Appropriate Use of Artificial Intelligence in Academic Settings
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that questions regarding guidelines for the appropriate use of artificial intelligence in academic settings and due process for academic misconduct in autonomous universities were addressed via a written reply on 24 September 2025. Members are directed to refer to the response to Question Nos 41 and 42 in the 24 September 2025 Order Paper for the full details. No new operational constraints, timelines, or specific eligibility rules were announced during this oral session.

results.title: ITE Students Benefitting from Public Service-ITE Internship Programme Since 2023
results.summary: Since its 2023 launch, the Public Service-ITE Internship Programme has benefited 284 Institute of Technical Education students across 41 agencies. The Public Service Division reports positive feedback and confirms plans to continue the programme. No specific future timeline or expansion details were provided in the response.

results.title: Impact of Cost Increases on Airlines' Operations at Changi Airport
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS) regulates aeronautical charges by Changi Airport Group (CAG) to maintain competitiveness. While global airlines face universal cost pressures from fuel, manpower, and inflation, the number of carriers at Changi Airport continues to grow. As of September 2025, approximately 100 airlines operate there, including nearly 20 low-cost carriers, with six new airlines joining in the last 12 months. CAAS and CAG will continue providing targeted rebates and incentives to support airline growth and mitigate cost challenges.

results.title: Imposing Limits to Credit Facilities and Charges by Platform Operators to Platform Workers Under Financing Programme
results.summary: The Minister for Law clarified that GFin Services, Grab's lending subsidiary, operates as an exempt moneylender under the Moneylenders Act with specific regulatory conditions. The Government imposes stringent restrictions on GFin, including legislated caps on loan quantum, limits on permitted fees, and debt service ratios that exceed standard licensed moneylender requirements. Additionally, GFin must implement internal credit scoring systems to assess maximum loan amounts and provide support initiatives for workers facing repayment challenges. No new measures to impose further limits or cap interest rates were proposed during the debate.

results.title: Improving Existing Poor-quality Cycling Paths
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority (LTA) aims to build connected, wide, and segregated cycling paths but acknowledges existing gaps due to staged construction or space constraints like mature trees. LTA is progressively upgrading older grey cycling paths in HDB towns to meet current red-coated standards of 2 metres width. Where widening is impossible, safety measures such as convex mirrors, speed regulating strips, and hedge trimming are implemented. For paths adjacent to pedestrian-only routes, path marking and signs guide users to stay on their respective lanes. Temporary connectivity gaps will be closed over time as new paths are built.

results.title: Increase in Fake Bulk Order Scams Impersonating SAF Personnel and Measures to Protect Small Businesses
results.summary: From May to 15 September 2025, the Police received at least six reports of fake bulk order scams impersonating Singapore Armed Forces (SAF) personnel, with a recent re-emergence of this variant since May 2025. Scammers pose as SAF officers to place bulk orders, then request additional items from fake suppliers, deceiving victims into making payments without delivery. As of 19 September 2025, six suspects were arrested, including a 22-year-old woman charged for relinquishing bank account control. The Police urge businesses to verify identities, avoid advance payments to new suppliers, and pay only upon delivery. Government agencies will not request payments to other businesses unless agreed via written contract. Businesses can verify authenticity using official "gov.sg" email addresses, the Singapore Government Directory, the ScamShield app, or the ScamShield Helpline at 1799.

results.title: Increasing Public Awareness of Measures to Support Singaporeans Re-entering Workforce
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower directed Members to refer to the reply to Question No 43 in the Order Paper for the Sitting on 23 September 2025 for details on increasing public awareness of measures supporting Singaporeans re-entering the workforce. This includes initiatives for older jobseekers, those seeking flexible work, and inclusive employers, as well as programmes for interview preparation and networking. No new measures or timelines were announced during this exchange.

results.title: Individuals in Severe Hoarding Cases Issued Treatment Orders Since March 2025
results.summary: The Mandatory Treatment Order framework under the Community Disputes Resolution (Amendment) Act 2024 has not yet come into operation as of the current date. Consequently, no individuals have been issued mandatory treatment orders or compliance bonds requiring psychiatric treatment since the Act's scheduled commencement on 24 March 2025. The Government is currently finalizing details to ensure the framework is effective and robust before implementation. Therefore, no data exists regarding the number of orders issued or the proportion of compliance.

results.title: Information and Breakdown of Local Sea-based Fish Farm Sector Workforce
results.summary: In 2024, the local sea-based fish farming sector employs approximately 400 workers, with about half being Singaporeans; however, data on the average age of local farmers is unavailable. To build a local talent pipeline, the Singapore Food Agency partners with Institutes of Higher Learning to offer pre-employment and continuing education programmes, exemplified by the April 2024 launch of a Specialist Diploma in Aquaculture Management and Technology with Temasek Polytechnic. Additionally, the agency facilitates job matching via WorkForce Singapore and the Employment and Employability Institute. To mitigate environmental risks and enhance resilience, the agency supports farms in upgrading capabilities through the Agri-Food Cluster Transformation Fund and strengthening biosecurity via Aquatic Animal Health Services.

results.title: Investigations into Alleged Impersonation of SAF Personnel Making Fraudulent Orders and Measures to Protect Small Businesses
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs directed Members to refer to the response to Question No. 3 delivered on 26 September 2025 regarding the progress of investigations into alleged impersonation of SAF personnel making fraudulent bulk orders with small businesses. The Minister also indicated that the Police's plans to engage small businesses with advice on necessary precautions to avoid falling victim to such situations were detailed in that same response. No new updates or specific timelines were provided in this session.

results.title: KPIs of Fund Managers under MAS' Equity Market Development Programme
results.summary: The $5 billion Equity Market Development Programme (EQDP) supports asset managers focused on Singapore public equities, particularly small and mid-cap stocks. Appointed managers are assessed over at least three years against three key performance indicators: mobilising third-party commercial capital, delivering investment returns benchmarked against relevant equity markets, and meeting developmental commitments to deepen their local presence and capabilities. The first three managers were appointed in July, with the Monetary Authority of Singapore on track to announce a second batch by year-end. These measures aim to broaden investor participation, amplify commercial capital inflows, and sustain growth and liquidity in Singapore's equities market.

results.title: Lessons to be Incorporated into Hawker Centres Upgrading Programme 2.0
results.summary: The National Environment Agency will apply insights from the Hawker Centres Transformation Programme to the Hawker Centres Upgrading Programme 2.0, focusing on upgrading older centres with ageing infrastructure into climate-resilient spaces. Key measures include exploring improved air circulation, ventilation, and ease of cleaning. Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centres built since 2015 are excluded from HUP 2.0 due to their newer infrastructure and will instead continue regular Repairs and Redecoration works. The Ministry will draw lessons from pilot sites like Geylang Serai and Cheng San to address infrastructural issues beyond typical repairs.

results.title: Making National Earth Observation Data from Singapore’s Satellites Accessible to Singapore-based Researchers
results.summary: Earth-observation data from Singapore Government and ST Engineering joint satellites is available via commercial arrangements with ST Engineering. Singapore-based researchers may apply for government research grants to cover access costs. The Minister stated this model balances data accessibility with commercial discipline, distinguishing it from free-data policies like Landsat or Copernicus. No direct government provision of free data was confirmed.

results.title: Measures to Enhance Protection against Fraudulent Bulk Order Scams Targeting Small Businesses
results.summary: The Minister referred Members to the response to Question No. 3 delivered on 26 September 2025 regarding measures to enhance protection against fraudulent bulk order scams targeting small businesses. This prior response addresses the specific queries on enhancing awareness, strengthening verification mechanisms, and providing support for victims. No new measures, timelines, or additional details were provided in this session. The parliamentary record indicates that the full policy details remain contained within the referenced earlier response.

results.title: Measuring Success of SGSecure Movement
results.summary: The Ministry measures SGSecure success via annual surveys published in the Singapore Terrorism Threat Assessment Report and tracks direct citizen engagement metrics, including business preparedness and programme participation. Social experiments conducted in June 2025 revealed a significant say-do gap: only 17% of passersby noticed simulated threats, with merely 5% of those intending to report them, contrasting sharply with 2024 survey figures showing 77% alertness and 88% reporting willingness. To address this perception gap, the Ministry acknowledges that no agency can prevent every attack and will refresh communications to maintain threat salience. Specific actions include launching dedicated SGSecure Instagram and TikTok accounts in 2024 and refreshing the campaign next year to better engage young Singaporeans.

results.title: Migrant Domestic Workers Fined for Illegal Moonlighting and Take-up Rate of Household Services Scheme
results.summary: Between 2020 and 2024, an average of 12 migrant domestic workers and 15 employers were fined annually for illegal moonlighting. Since the Household Services Scheme (HSS) became permanent in 2021, participating companies rose from 80 to 240, with hourly rates ranging from $18 to $30, significantly lower than the $1,000 to $1,300 monthly cost of hiring a full-time worker. The Ministry confirmed that HSS companies may offer online booking services and will collaborate with NTUC to strengthen public awareness. While the Member requested expansion beyond current operators and improved accessibility, the Ministry stated that interested companies can write to MOM to join the scheme and determine their own booking methods.

results.title: Monitoring of Commercial-vacancy Durations and Concentration by Landlords
results.summary: The Minister for National Development stated that the question regarding commercial-vacancy monitoring, landlord rent strategies, rental affordability impacts, and the feasibility of a vacancy tax has already been addressed in the reply to Parliamentary Question No 60 on the Order Paper dated 23 September 2025. No new measures, timelines, or operational constraints were introduced during this session. Consequently, the specific details on vacancy duration monitoring, rent concentration assessments, and public disclosure mandates remain as previously outlined in that referenced response.

results.title: Monitoring of Employers to Protect Jobseekers and Participants of Traineeship Programmes
results.summary: Members queried eligibility criteria for host organisations under the GRaduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT) Programme and the Global Talent Ready Programme, specifically regarding recent retrenchments and audit mechanisms for employers using grants to displace staff. The Minister for Manpower directed members to refer to the combined reply issued on 23 September 2025 concerning graduate employment and the GRIT programme, which addressed these inquiries. No new measures, timelines, or specific audit mechanisms were announced during this session.

results.title: More Initiatives for Infants and Toddlers Under Grow Well SG and Assessing Long-term Impact on Children's Development
results.summary: Grow Well SG initiatives for infants and toddlers prioritize educating parents, including first-time parents, on age-appropriate healthy behaviours through the Childhood Health Behaviours checklist integrated into developmental screenings from age 0 to 6. Support resources are accessible via Parent Hub, Parenting for Wellness, Families for Life, and ECDA's Beanstalk, while the revised Early Years Development Framework guides preschool educators to promote overall well-being. To address digital challenges, the programme will enhance support for managing children's screen time amidst growing social media prevalence. Impact assessment relies on qualitative indicators covering children's lifestyle habits—specifically screen time, physical activity, and sleep—alongside surveys measuring parental awareness to refine future initiatives.

results.title: Number of AMD-related Fires in HDB Flats and Individuals Penalised for Using Non-compliant AMDs or Batteries
results.summary: Over the last five years, 187 fires involving Active Mobility Devices (AMDs) occurred in HDB estates, representing about 4% of total HDB fires. The Land Transport Authority detected over 6,000 cases of non-compliant AMDs on public paths and more than 100 retailer violations. To enhance safety, the Active Mobility Fire Safety Taskforce will intensify public education under the "Be Device-Safe" programme regarding non-compliant device dangers and safe charging. The Ministry is also considering tougher enforcement actions to deter the use and sale of non-compliant AMDs, which pose significant safety risks.

results.title: Percentage of Support Disbursed under Existing Schemes Going to Micro and Small Enterprises Versus Medium Enterprises
results.summary: The Minister stated that IMDA's CTO-as-a-Service platform and the Productivity Solutions Grant (PSG) facilitate AI adoption for SMEs, with the PSG specifically designed for accessibility by small and micro enterprises. In 2024, nearly 3,000 SMEs, including micro enterprises, adopted AI-enabled solutions through these grants. Additionally, the Heartland Enterprise Centre Singapore (HECS) provides training programmes to equip heartland merchants with basic digital skills and common AI tools, while referring those seeking advanced applications to relevant partners. The response did not specify the exact percentage of support disbursed to micro and small enterprises versus medium enterprises.

results.title: Pickleball-related Noise Complaints in Residential Estates and Measures to Balance Recreation with Residents' Rest
results.summary: The Minister for National Development directed Members to refer to the Ministry of National Development's reply dated 25 September 2025 regarding a similar query on pickleball noise. This response addresses the number of complaints received between 2024 and 2025, measures taken by Town Councils to balance recreation with residents' rest, and the status of potential nationwide guidelines or infrastructure adjustments like quieter equipment. Specific statistics on complaint volumes and details on current operational measures were not restated in this session but are available in the referenced prior reply.

results.title: Plans and Guidelines to Establish Religious Prayer Rooms in Organisations
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed that the Ministry does not currently provide guidelines to organisations or entities wishing to establish religious prayer rooms. No plans were announced for the Ministry to develop such guidelines in the future. Consequently, there is no stated government framework or operational constraint regarding the creation of these spaces within organisations at this time.

results.title: Plans for National-level AI Task Force to Create Solutions and Make Global Impact
results.summary: The Government does not plan to establish a centralised national-level AI task force, as the refreshed National AI Strategy 2.0 promotes distributed ownership and capability development across key stakeholders. Instead, sectoral initiatives like the Manufacturing AI Centre of Excellence convene industry, research, and startup partners to develop specific solutions for use cases such as predictive maintenance and operations optimisation. The Government will continue supporting industry adoption by helping discover off-the-shelf AI solutions or developing new ones tailored to specific sectors, rather than creating a single centralised entity.

results.title: Plans to Make Classification of Etomidate as Class C Drug Permanent
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs has temporarily listed etomidate as a Class C drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act from 1 September 2025 to 28 February 2026 due to urgent enforcement needs, pending robust scientific evidence of long-term addictiveness. While the substance is not yet proscribed under United Nations Drug Conventions, the Government acknowledges serious adverse effects including suicidal ideation, confusion, irregular heart rhythms, and potential fatality. The Ministry plans to discuss with international counterparts how to adapt legislation for emerging substances like etomidate and vapes, ensuring enforcement powers remain relevant while awaiting conclusive data on long-term harm. A permanent classification requires sufficient evidence of significant harm and addictiveness before listing.

results.title: Plans to Trial On-road Cycling Lanes in More Residential Neighbourhoods
results.summary: The Ministry has trialled on-road cycling lanes at Tanah Merah Coast Road (full day) and West Camp Road (time-based), noting that future implementation requires low vehicular traffic, wide roads, and an existing critical mass of cyclists. While acknowledging land constraints in high-density Singapore, the Minister stated the focus is expanding off-road infrastructure via the Islandwide Cycling Network programme, which aims to increase the cycling path network from 750km to approximately 1,300km by 2030 across all HDB towns. Additionally, the 21.5km North South Corridor will serve as a trunk route connecting the Northern region to the city. The transcript does not confirm plans for new on-road trials in residential neighbourhoods beyond the stated criteria.

results.title: Policy Enhancements for Priority Seating on Public Transport and Considerations for Legal Enforcement
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport declined to introduce legal stipulations for priority seating, citing that legislation should not crowd out gracious conduct. Instead, the Ministry focuses on fostering a caring commuter culture through the Caring SG Commuter Movement and publicity campaigns to encourage voluntary seat offers for the elderly, disabled, and those with invisible medical conditions. A 2024 Public Transport Council survey indicates that over 70% of respondents with disabilities found it easier to secure a seat compared to the previous year. The Ministry will continue building on this positive momentum rather than implementing new legal constraints.

results.title: Prioritising Green Modes of Transport in Tengah and Other New Towns
results.summary: Tengah is designed as a "car-lite" district featuring bus lanes on both sides of roads and cycling paths currently under progressive completion. Seven bus services, including a City Direct Service, serve the town, while the upcoming Jurong Region Line will connect residents to key destinations like Jurong Lake District and NTU via interchanges at Choa Chu Kang, Boon Lay, and Jurong East. The Land Transport Authority is studying the feasibility of a new Tengah Line for longer-term connectivity. Specific timelines for completing cycling paths and launching the new line were not stated in the response.

results.title: Promoting Use of Advanced Technologies and Software Solutions to Boost Productivity in Construction Sector
results.summary: The Building and Construction Authority (BCA) actively promotes technology adoption among construction firms, including SMEs, through the Built Environment Technology and Capability (BETC) Grant to build technological capabilities and the Productivity Solutions Grant (PSG) to co-fund digital solutions. These schemes support the implementation of Building Information Modelling (BIM) software for 3D planning, coordination, and material estimation to avoid abortive work, optimise costs, and ensure timely deliveries. The BCA's efforts aim to boost productivity across the sector by enhancing firms' digital capabilities and operational efficiency.

results.title: Proposal to Allow Persons under 18 to Access Counselling or Screening without Parental Consent
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) has convened an expert group to study whether parental consent is required for minors under 18 accessing Tier 2 low-intensity mental health services, such as counselling by licensed practitioners, while retaining consent requirements for higher-risk interventions like medication. Key stakeholders including parents, youths, and service providers were engaged to shape guidelines that balance parental involvement with the need to remove barriers for minors in sensitive situations. The Ministry expects to conclude stakeholder consultations and publish a framework or guidelines by early next year, which will provide clarity on when parental consent may be waived and how service providers should navigate these circumstances. The study findings will be made public upon completion of the guidelines.

results.title: Protocols and Metrics Used to Synchronise HDB's Housing Plans with LTA's Timelines for Rail and Bus Network Expansion
results.summary: Singapore employs an integrated master planning approach to synchronise HDB housing developments with LTA transport timelines, ensuring infrastructure aligns with population growth. Agencies initiate long-gestation projects years in advance to coordinate road, water, and telecommunications provision with housing supply. HDB towns are planned in tandem with transport networks, with agencies collaborating to stage infrastructure and development plans within towns. Where feasible, facilities and services are delivered concurrently with housing completion to prevent transport lagging behind public housing expansion.

results.title: Provision of High Chairs for Children at NEA-run Hawker Centres
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that some hawker associations have already provided high chairs at National Environment Agency-managed hawker centres based on localized needs. No specific government mandate or future timeline for universal provision was announced. The response indicates that current arrangements are driven by individual association decisions rather than a centralized policy directive.

results.title: Public Education on End-of-life Conversations in Light of Ageing Population
results.summary: In 2023, multiple agencies launched a multi-year legacy planning campaign featuring islandwide roadshows, mobile clinics, and workshops to promote tools like Advance Care Planning, CPF nominations, LPAs, and Wills. In July, the Ministry introduced myACP, a free digital tool for documenting long-term care preferences. Concurrently, relevant agencies collaborate on public education to guide families on post-death matters, utilizing My Legacy@LifeSG as a one-stop Government portal for information on end-of-life planning, estate settlement, and funeral arrangements.

results.title: Pursuing Israel's Accountability for Violation of International Humanitarian Law Beyond Diplomatic Statements
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs and Senior Minister of State for Foreign Affairs addressed the query regarding specific international mechanisms for pursuing Israel's accountability in their Ministerial Statements on the Situation in the Middle East delivered on 22 September 2025. These statements covered Singapore's public position on humanitarian aid violations but did not detail specific sanctions, judicial referrals, or coordinated actions within the United Nations, ASEAN, or the Commonwealth. Consequently, the transcript does not provide the requested information on available mechanisms beyond diplomatic statements.

results.title: Raising Public Awareness on Negative Impact of Selling and Consuming Endangered Stingray Species
results.summary: The National Parks Board (NParks) regulates trade for CITES-listed species but notes that the whitespotted whipray and sharpnose whipray are not currently listed. Consequently, the Government has no specific plans to further educate suppliers and consumers regarding these particular stingrays. Instead, NParks collaborates with nature groups to raise general conservation awareness and advises consumers to verify the CITES status of seafood origins before purchase.

results.title: Rationale for Maintaining Distinctions in Support Schemes between Single-parent Unwed Household and Married Couples
results.summary: The Minister clarified that benefits like the Working Mother's Child Relief and Parenthood Tax Rebate are restricted to married couples to support parenthood within marriage, while HDB flats are primarily set aside for families. However, the Ministry ensures single unwed parents are not penalised by extending child-focused subsidies—including education, healthcare, childcare, and the Child Development Account—to all Citizen children regardless of parental marital status. For housing, single unwed parents aged 21 and above may purchase up to a 3-room flat or a resale flat, while those unable to buy may be considered for public rental flats. Additional financial assistance and holistic case management are available through social service offices and family service centres upon request.

results.title: Recycling Rate for Glass Waste
results.summary: In 2024, glass waste constituted approximately one per cent of Singapore's total waste, achieving a recycling rate of 7%. The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment clarified that laminated glass from end-of-life vehicles and construction and demolition waste is currently not recycled. This exclusion stems from existing operational constraints and a lack of commercial viability. Consequently, no specific percentage of recycled glass waste comprises these laminated materials at this time.

results.title: Refund Demands on Ineligible Recipients of Self-Employed Person Income Relief Scheme
results.summary: Approximately 2,200 Self-Employed Person Income Relief Scheme (SIRS) recipients, representing around 1% of all recipients, were found ineligible following comprehensive audits that verified declarations on trade income and property ownership. The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) has progressively contacted these individuals to request refunds after completing audit phases, noting that some refund demands were issued only after 2023 due to the time required for thorough back-end checks. While MOM will seek recovery from all ineligible recipients to preserve scheme integrity, it will consider individual circumstances during appeals. The Minister did not specify the exact reasons for the delays in refund demands or confirm whether cash flow impacts were explicitly considered in the recovery process.

results.title: Rejected Flexible Work Arrangement Requests Since December 2024
results.summary: Since the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests (TG-FWAR) took effect in December 2024, the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices (TAFEP) received one complaint in July 2025 regarding a verbal communication of an FWA outcome instead of written notice. TAFEP assisted the company in revising its processes to comply with the guidelines. Employers facing implementation challenges or employees whose requests are not properly considered may approach TAFEP for assistance. Detailed statistics on rejected FWA requests and specific grounds for rejection since December 2024 were not provided in this response.

results.title: Requirement for Verification of Account Number and Accountholder Name during Fund Transfers
results.summary: MAS is reviewing account number and name matching approaches after studying international experiences. Under the current system, erroneous transfers are resolved by the sender contacting their bank, which engages the recipient's bank to initiate a refund. If the recipient refuses or is unresponsive, the bank advises next steps, potentially including a Police report, as retaining erroneous funds is illegal. Clear protocols guide banks in these situations. Consumers are urged to confirm account details before transferring and remain vigilant against fraudulent demands, noting that Government officials will never request money transfers or banking log-in details.

results.title: Research on Social Interactions within Neighbourhoods with Newer HDB Town Designs
results.summary: The Ministry for National Development confirmed that the Housing and Development Board (HDB) conducts the Sample Household Survey every five years, with the latest 2021 results indicating 97% of residents perceive sufficient spaces for neighbourly interaction. Data suggests that newer block designs remain as effective as older ones in facilitating social engagement, with most interactions occurring in common spaces like community decks. To support this, HDB has introduced Community Living Rooms with seating since 2014 and provides 3-Generation playgrounds in newer precincts to integrate children, adults, and seniors. Additionally, precinct pavilions are available for larger social functions and community activities within newer HDB estates.

results.title: Response to Findings that Singapore is Most Expensive City in Asia for Locals and Expatriates
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister clarified that a Lee Kuan Yew School of Public Policy study, based on data up to Q1 2025, ranked Singapore as the fourth most expensive city for expatriates and 28th for residents in Asia. Since then, core inflation has moderated to 0.3% year-on-year in August 2025, down from 2.8% in 2024. To assist households, the Government introduced Budget 2025 measures including $800 in Community Development Council vouchers for all households and enhanced U-Save rebates for HDB residents. Additionally, the one-off SG60 Package provides $600 in vouchers for adults and $800 for seniors. Beyond transitory support, the Government has strengthened social safety nets in housing, healthcare, education, and retirement while monitoring cost of living to provide further assistance where necessary.

results.title: Response to Singapore's Drop in 2025 World Talent Ranking
results.summary: The Government attributes Singapore's drop to seventh place in the IMD World Talent Ranking 2025 primarily to perception-based factors rather than actual performance declines. Key drivers include reduced executive confidence in apprenticeships, employee training prioritisation, and the ability to retain talent amidst demographic constraints limiting workforce growth. While public education expenditure as a percentage of GDP fell to 65th, the Minister clarified that absolute spending and outcomes remain strong, evidenced by a 17th-place ranking in expenditure per student and top-tier PISA scores. The ranking decline was not caused by low education investment, as total budget allocation and initiatives like SkillsFuture continue to be substantial, though perception metrics on skilled labour availability and worker motivation negatively impacted the overall score.

results.title: Review on CHAS Visit Cap for Chronic and Mental Health Follow-up Care at Specialist Outpatient Clinics Versus GP Clinics
results.summary: The Ministry of Health clarified that a strict cost parity between Specialist Outpatient Clinics (SOCs) and General Practitioner (GP) clinics under the Community Health Assist Scheme (CHAS) is not feasible due to fundamentally different funding models. GPs are private providers receiving capped subsidies for common conditions, whereas SOCs are public institutions funded by a percentage of bills with no visit or annual maximum caps, leading to inherently varying costs. Consequently, the Ministry maintains that patients requiring specialised assessment should remain at SOCs, while medically stable cases are discharged to primary care for management, regardless of the resulting subsidy disparity. The differential in subsidies between the two settings is an inevitable outcome of these distinct operational structures.

results.title: Reviewing Curricula and Internship Programmes of IHLs to Ensure Employability and Preparedness for Workforce
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) regularly review curricula and internship programmes in consultation with sector agencies and industry partners to align with evolving job market needs. Current frameworks equip students with baseline digital literacy, AI competencies, and hands-on experience using industry-relevant tools, exemplified by Temasek Polytechnic's FutureX - Digital Technologies & AI Centre. Beyond technical skills, IHLs are increasing curricular focus on critical thinking, collaboration, and cross-cultural abilities to prepare graduates for roles that complement AI. As of June 2025, approximately 30,000 entry-level jobs are available across sectors like Health & Social Sciences and Professional Services. The Ministry will continue collaborating with institutions to ensure graduates possess the necessary skills for an evolving workforce.

results.title: Reviewing Individual Requests to Exceed MediSave Withdrawal Limits
results.summary: Individual requests to exceed MediSave withdrawal limits are reviewed case-by-case, assessing patients' ability to afford expenses, available balances, and eligibility for other financing like MediFund. Medical social workers in public healthcare institutions guide patients on exemptions and support avenues, ensuring treatment priority regardless of financial circumstances. While processing times vary by case complexity, no patient is denied medically indicated treatment due to finances. The Ministry periodically reviews limits, recently extending usage for outpatient treatments and scans, and remains open to feedback on further enhancements to balance accessibility with long-term fund sufficiency.

results.title: Reviewing Policy on Non-air-conditioned Class B2 and Class C Wards in Public Hospitals Given Rising Temperatures Due To Climate Change
results.summary: The Ministry of Health will continue to work with public hospitals to assess ventilation and cooling adequacy in non-air-conditioned Class B2 and Class C wards, balancing affordability and infection control. Currently, hospitals can deploy hybrid cooling, portable air coolers, additional fans, and air purifiers to enhance comfort during sustained heat waves or severe haze. The Minister stated that while a full policy review is not explicitly confirmed, the Ministry will monitor conditions and implement measures as needed to ensure patient and staff welfare without specifying a timeline for installing air-conditioning.

results.title: Rooftop Solar Panel Installation Rates across Property Types over Past Five Years
results.summary: Currently, 6% of private residential properties in Singapore have rooftop solar panel installations. Grid-connected solar capacity and installation numbers for both private residential and non-residential properties have risen steadily over the past five years. While specific data for private non-residential properties is unavailable due to current data collection methodology, trends indicate consistent growth across public and private non-residential sectors. The Minister provided these statistics without detailing specific annual figures or capacity breakdowns in the transcript.

results.title: Sea-based Fish Farms that Had Received Government Grants Prior to Farm Closure
results.summary: Of the 26 sea-based fish farms that exited the industry in 2024, six had received close to $700,000 in total grants to enhance productivity, with all recipients meeting grant conditions. The Singapore Food Agency continues partnering with farms to improve commercial viability through biosecurity support via Aquatic Animal Health Services and capability upgrades through the Agri-Food Cluster Transformation Fund. Despite a decrease in farm numbers, sector productivity increased from 38.6 to 40.7 tonnes per hectare per year between 2023 and 2024, reflecting a shift toward higher efficiency.

results.title: Simplifying Access to Home Caregiving Grant by Enabling Medical Social Workers or Doctors to Initiate Applications
results.summary: Medical social workers and general practitioners can already initiate Home Caregiving Grant (HCG) applications for eligible caregivers during healthcare encounters, with back-end verification handled subsequently. The Agency of Integrated Care assists care recipients undergoing severe disability assessments in identifying relevant schemes, while Silver Generation Ambassadors continue to share long-term care information during home visits. The Ministry has recently implemented these improvements to broaden access to financial support schemes without introducing new procedural changes.

results.title: Statistics on Age Discrimination Complaints Lodged with TAFEP and Sectors Most Frequently Implicated
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) and the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices (TAFEP) received an average of 61 age discrimination complaints annually in 2023 and 2024, with administrative and support services, wholesale and retail trade, and accommodation and food services being the most frequently implicated sectors. Beyond formal complaints, TAFEP proactively identified an average of 90 potentially discriminatory job advertisements on MyCareersFuture in the same period and engaged employers to amend them. The second Workplace Fairness Bill is expected to be tabled by year-end, with the Workplace Fairness Act targeted for implementation in 2026 or 2027 if passed. Annual fair employment practice reports indicate no uptrend in complaints over the past five years, averaging 77 cases from 2018 to 2022.

results.title: Statistics on HDB Estates' Zebra Crossings Converted to Courtesy Crossings
results.summary: Between 2022 and 2024, the Housing and Development Board converted 37 zebra crossings in HDB estates to courtesy crossings. This initiative addresses safety concerns where pedestrians felt a false sense of security due to limited motorist visibility, particularly near right-angled turns. The policy rationale involves replacing these crossings with courtesy crossings, often complemented by traffic calming measures like road humps, to encourage greater caution from both pedestrians and motorists. The Ministry aims to ensure resident safety by reminding pedestrians to check surroundings and motorists to slow down, rather than relying on fixed zebra crossing infrastructure in specific locations.

results.title: Strengthening SCDF's Emergency Response Capabilities and Enhancing Community Engagement for Fire Outbreaks
results.summary: The Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) strengthens emergency response through the Responder Plus Programme, offering free training on fire extinguishers, CPR, and AEDs under the SGSecure movement. Jointly with the Temasek Foundation, HDB, and Town Councils, SCDF has installed fire extinguishers at Lifesavers' Emergency Points in lift lobbies of one in every two residential HDB blocks since October 2023. Over 5,000 such points are operational, covering more than 90% of targeted blocks, with the full rollout scheduled for completion by 2028. Regular block evacuation exercises continue to be conducted by SCDF and the People's Association.

results.title: Students in Local Universities Offered Structured Internships which Transitioned into Full-time Employment
results.summary: Based on the 2024 Joint Autonomous University Graduate Employment Survey (JAUGES), approximately 40% of graduates received job offers from internship companies, with about 60% accepting these roles. Full-time employment transitions depend on available positions and intern performance assessments, though the Ministry of Education does not track the specific sector split between private and public employers. The primary objective remains providing on-the-job training and soft skills development rather than guaranteeing employment. The Ministry is not explicitly stated to be expanding internship-to-job pathways in this response.

results.title: Studies on Public Perceptions on Wakes and After-death Arrangements
results.summary: The Ministry for Sustainability and the Environment confirmed it conducts periodic studies on after-death practices to inform policy reviews but stated there is currently no active study on public perceptions of wakes. Consequently, no timeline for publicly communicating insights or initiating community conversations on this specific topic has been provided. The Minister indicated that any future research would be commissioned as needed to support policy development rather than as a scheduled initiative.

results.title: Supporting Home Hospice Services
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) supports home hospice services through regular funding rate adjustments, which were increased this year, alongside initiatives like the Equipment Rental Scheme and a standardised compassionate discharge protocol. These measures aim to improve productivity and seamless transitions from public hospitals. Capacity has risen from 2,400 to 3,000 patients over two years, with a target of 3,600 by year-end. Total Government funding will continue to grow to match rising costs and patient numbers. The Minister confirmed that besides direct funding, support extends to worker training, hiring, and technology adoption under the National Strategy for Palliative Care.

results.title: Take-up Rate and Outcomes of ComLink+ Progress and Employment Packages Since Launch
results.summary: The ComLink+ Packages for Preschool and Employment were launched in August and December 2024 respectively, with Home Ownership and Debt Clearance packages rolling out in May and June 2025. As of end-June 2025, approximately 2,200 preschool-aged children and 1,600 families are enrolled in the Preschool and Employment packages. The Minister noted that not all eligible families are immediately ready to participate as they prioritize other action plan items, with family coaches monitoring progress and facilitating enrolment when families are prepared. The Ministry is currently monitoring take-up rates for the newer packages and intends to analyse overall impact, with a future update to be provided. Specific outcomes such as reduction in absentee days have not yet been identified due to the early implementation stage.

results.title: Time Limit for Minister's Speech
results.summary: Members moved to exempt proceedings on the item under discussion from Standing Order 48(8), which mandates a time limit for Minister Chan Chun Sing's speech. This request seeks general assent to remove the specific time constraint applicable to the Minister's address. The motion aims to allow an extended duration for the Minister's speech without adhering to the standard parliamentary time limits. No further details regarding the speech content or specific duration were provided in the transcript.

results.title: Total Public Funding Committed to Healthcare Research
results.summary: Over the past five years, $2.4 billion in public funding has been committed to healthcare research under national plans. Approximately 50% of this total supports research on chronic diseases, including cancer, cardiovascular conditions, diabetes, stroke, and mental health. The remaining funds are allocated to research talent development and artificial intelligence in healthcare. The Ministry tracks distribution across these key areas, covering rehabilitation and ageing-related conditions within the broader chronic disease and non-disease categories.

results.title: Tracking Employment Outcomes, Job Transitions and Promotions of Individuals who Have Completed SkillsFuture-funded Training
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry has already addressed the query regarding the tracking of employment outcomes, job transitions, and promotions for SkillsFuture-funded training participants in an oral reply to Question No 20 on 25 September 2025. Consequently, no new measures or data publication plans regarding post-training wage progression or career mobility were announced during this session. The specific details of the previously provided response remain the authoritative source for this matter.

results.title: Traditional Election Advertisement Rule Contravention for Grassroots Advisers' Promotional Material for GE2025
results.summary: Promotional material featuring grassroots advisers (GRAs) maintained during the 2025 General Elections campaign period contravenes traditional election advertisement (TEA) rules only if it is intended to promote or procure electoral success for a candidate or party. When a GRA runs as a candidate, pre-existing displays bearing their photograph constitute TEA if they reasonably appeal for continued support to serve as a Member of Parliament, requiring compliance with election advertising rules.
